chunk_summary: This section outlines dashboard views for managing overdue high-risk children and failed checkpoints in tele-nutrition workflows, emphasizing actionable insights for clinicians to improve care delivery and safety monitoring.
learning_objectives: Understand the design and functionality of dashboards for high-risk children in tele-nutrition services.
Learn how to identify and manage overdue cases, failed checkpoints, and escalation verification.
Explore the integration of structured templates and follow-up tracking to enhance telecare safety.
Recognize the importance of linking failed contact cases to community services or alternative outreach.
key_facts: Dashboards should answer three daily questions: who is overdue, who is worsening, and who needs escalation completion verified.
View 1: High-risk overdue list filters by risk tier, measurement confidence, and next due date, with one-click contact logging and a 'failed contact count' field.
View 2: Failed checkpoint list flags growth faltering (2-4 weeks), acute malnutrition (2 weeks), anemia (no hemoglobin rise), and obesity (4-6 weeks).
View 3: Escalation verification tracks in-person measurements, lab tests, or referrals, marking unverified cases as telecare safety failures.
A 'lost to follow-up' status is added after repeated failed contacts to maintain accurate denominators in quality metrics.
Initial implementation can start with three spreadsheet tabs matching the dashboard views, reviewed weekly.
reference_quote: Design dashboards as action lists, not as retrospective analytics reports. (Source: WHO Guideline: recommendations on digital interventions for health system strengthening)
document_key_topics: Management of high-risk and overdue cases
